items.title: The rugged country that holds the secrets of Dezi Freeman's final days

children.content: The fugitive resurfaced more than 150 kilometres away from the town where he shot and killed two police officers seven months ago. Mystified locals are questioning how.

articles.title: 'Evil inside him': Dezi Freeman's nephew speaks out on cop killer uncle's death

children.content: When Dezi Freeman was shot dead, it left his family with mixed emotions. Some are shattered, others know an "evil man" is dead. His nephew speaks out.

image.altText: Two men smiling for a photo in a backyard.

articles.title: Freeman death stirs shock, sadness, anger in scarred Porepunkah

children.content: Seven months after a gunman killed two police officers on a nearby property, the tiny town woke to news Dezi Freeman was dead.

image.altText: A man in a dark jacket and pants holding a phone to his ear walks past a brick building that says "Punkah Pantry".

articles.title: What we know about where Dezi Freeman made his last stand

children.content: The rural property where police finally caught up with the fugitive gunman was a good place to disappear.

image.altText: A black police armourned vehicle is parked next to a shipping container. A black pipe is connected between the two.

gallery.altText: A road leading through Thologolong, with a blue and white sign beside the tree-lined road.

thumbnail.altText: A road leading through Thologolong, with a blue and white sign beside the tree-lined road.

items.title: Freeman joins a long line of fugitives captured or killed in Australia

children.content: Australia has a long history of violent outlaws heading bush to avoid capture. But how is it still possible to remain on the run for so long?

image.altText: A photo of Dezi and an image of Ned Kelly

items.title: Phone ban in classrooms extended to include smartwatches, headphones

children.content: Victorian students in public and private schools will be banned from wearing smartwatches with internet connectivity and using headphones in classrooms from next year after the government announced an extension of the device ban in schools.

image.altText: Close up of a smart watch, as worn by a seven-year-old boy.

items.title: Remote Australians angered by Vodafone ad claiming 'nothing's out here'

children.content: A new Vodafone campaign has regional Australians seething after the telecommunications giant claimed to focus its network infrastructure on where "98 whatever per cent of you actually live".

gallery.altText: A screengrab of an ad campaign featuring an emu standing in the outback next to a woman in a red dress

thumbnail.altText: A screengrab of an ad campaign featuring an emu standing in the outback next to a woman in a red dress

items.title: House prices rise everywhere besides Sydney and Melbourne

children.content: There is a significant divide between property price growth in major cities as buyers weigh up interest rate hikes and geopolitical tensions.

image.altText: A man and a woman smile at the camera.

items.title: 13yo girl charged after alleged crime rampage in Melbourne

children.content: Victoria Police say the teenager was driving a stolen car when she swerved towards a group of people from the Jewish community on Saturday.

image.altText: Crime scene tape outside a home.

items.title: Melbourne couple allegedly beat and starved woman enslaved in their home

children.content: A court has heard an Indonesian woman was allegedly threatened and coerced to provide domestic services while living with a Melbourne couple.

image.altText: A courtroom sketch shows a man scowling and a woman with a blank expression.

items.title: From Porepunkah to Thologolong: The long search for Dezi Freeman

children.content: When the parents of Senior Constable Vadim de Waart-Hottart flew to Melbourne for a police service, little did they know their son's killer would emerge just days later.

image.altText: An image showing a middle-aged man staring down the barrel of the camera.

items.title: Cobram Estate completes $260m purchase of huge US olive grove

children.content: Australia's largest olive oil producer has completed its purchase of a massive olive grove in California, giving it about 70 per cent of the state's production.

gallery.altText: A man in a shirt and reaches up to some olives on a tree in an olive grove.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a shirt and reaches up to some olives on a tree in an olive grove.

avatar.alt: A profile image of a man with short, brown hair.

items.title: Melbourne childcare centre suspended for 90 days following multiple breaches

children.content: Victoria's new childcare watchdog has suspended operations at Milestones Early Learning Werribee after identifying multiple 'serious and concerning' issues.

image.altText: Milestones early learning centre

items.title: Victims of Ponzi scheming accountant lament 'manifestly inadequate' sentence

children.content: An accountant from Mildura in Victoria's north-west found guilty of an egregious breach of trust has avoided a lengthy prison term, to the dismay of his clients.

image.altText: A man in a white t shirt drinking a green beverage from a glass

items.title: Plans to increase on-street parking fees, fines and rates in Melbourne draft budget

children.content: The City of Melbourne's 2026-27 draft budget proposes an increase to on-street parking fees despite Lord Mayor Nick Reece's election promise to not raise fees.

image.altText: Princes Bridge near Flinders St Station in Melbourne's CBD with hardly any traffic on it.

items.title: Alleged Melbourne brothel killer to stand trial over sex worker's death

children.content: Michael James Chalmers is ordered to face trial after pleading not guilty to murdering a sex worker at a Melbourne brothel in 2024.

image.altText: A man wearing a black singlet at a nightclub holds a drink

items.title: Banned Xerri reveals Essendon abuse leading to blood-wiping incident

children.content: Kangaroos ruckman Tristan Xerri gives evidence at the AFL tribunal as he accepts a suspension for rubbing blood from his nose on the face of Essendon captain Andrew McGrath.

gallery.altText: Tristan Xerri rubs blood on the face of Andrew McGrath

thumbnail.altText: Tristan Xerri rubs blood on the face of Andrew McGrath

items.title: Passengers fear further V/Line overcrowding as free travel begins

children.content: Commuters say they are worried overcrowding on V/Line train services could worsen as free travel begins for Victorians to ease the pain of the fuel crisis.

image.altText: The interior of a full train carriage showing full seats and the aisle full of standing passengers

items.title: Liberals to re-run preselection after blocking candidate who beat Deeming

children.content: The Victorian Liberals will re-run a preselection battle involving controversial MP Moira Deeming after the party blocked the candidate who won top spot.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit smiles for the camera in front of a colourful background.

items.title: Former AFL player Nick Stevens found guilty of dishonesty charges

children.content: A jury has found AFL player Nick Stevens guilty of deceptively charging families for swimming pools they never received.

image.altText: man walking outside with phone in hand

items.title: Storm forward hospitalised after suspected stroke in 'medical episode'

children.content: The Melbourne Storm say Tui Kamikamica is receiving the best possible care after being rushed to a hospital following a "medical episode" this morning.

image.altText: Tui Kamikamica carries the ball

items.title: Coalition, One Nation idea would be 'absolute disaster' for Victoria

children.content: Former premier Steve Bracks says One Nation teaming up with the Coalition would be a disaster for Victoria, and has hit out at Jeff Kennett for even suggesting it.

avatar.alt: Richard Willingham in front of a white background, stares straight down the barrel.

items.title: Next six months to be 'horrific': Food charities hit by twin crises

children.content: Charities supplying food relief to struggling Australians are grappling with the twin pressures of rising demand for help while their own fuel bills soar.

image.altText: A woman in a navy cap and white shirt stands beside the open door of a red car holding a silver box.

items.title: Kylie Minogue to lead AFL grand final entertainment, ending Aussie drought

children.content: Pop superstar Kylie Minogue has been named as the men's AFL grand final pre-match entertainment, the first time since 2021 that an Australian artist has headlined the event.

image.altText: Kylie Minogue sings with her arms in the air in a black and white dress.

items.title: Travelling on the Spirit of Tasmania ferries is about to become more expensive

children.content: Customers booking a Bass Strait crossing from tomorrow will be hit with a 15 per cent surcharge. The government says it's to help ferry operator TT-line deal with rising fuel costs.

image.altText: A large white and red ship docked in Hobart, with a silhouette of a large building in front of it.

items.title: Public transport to be free for Victorians in April as fuel prices climb

children.content: The Victorian government says public transport will be free across the state in April in a bid to relieve cost-of-living pressure as fuel prices continue to climb.

image.altText: A train stops at a station while commuters wait on the platform alongside it.

items.title: The next step after treaty is here — and there's a lot at stake

children.content: Victoria's treaty elections are underway, with successful candidates taking on new responsibilities. But uncertainty remains, with the opposition sticking to its pledge to scrap the agreement if elected in November.

items.title: Victorian government ordered to pay landowner $27m over rezoning

children.content: A court orders the Victorian government to pay more than $27 million in compensation to a landowner whose property was partially rezoned for public acquisition, after initially making the man a formal offer of $0.

image.altText: Exterior of a court.

items.title: Former AFL footballer asked how much he drank before he was allegedly raped

children.content: Daniel Hayes was 17 years old when he was allegedly raped by his coach while playing in an Australian Rules football talent league in Victoria.

image.altText: An Indigenous man in front of a wooden fence with mottled light over his face.

items.title: Late wickets give South Australia edge in absorbing Shield final

children.content: South Australia strikes twice late on day four to snatch the momentum from Victoria in the Sheffield Shield final at Melbourne's Junction Oval.

image.altText: Three South Australian players celebrate a Victorian wicket in the Sheffield Shield final.

items.title: Police search for teens after Melbourne crash as paramedic injured

children.content: Police say four teenagers in a stolen car collided with an older couple in Carlton this morning, with two fleeing the scene immediately.

image.altText: Blue and white police tape at a crime scene.

items.title: AFL players to lose insurance coverage for brain injuries

children.content: More than 500 AFL players will soon no longer be covered by insurance for brain injuries in their superannuation after Zurich Insurance moved to cut their ability to claim for concussion and head trauma.

articles.title: Magpies confirm 'hamstring awareness' for Moore after win over Giants

children.content: Collingwood is expecting to be without its skipper Darcy Moore and his predecessor Scott Pendlebury next week against Brisbane after the pair were hobbled during the win over GWS.

image.altText: Darcy Moore heads into the locker rooms with an injury

articles.title: AFL admits crucial umpire error late in Crows' defeat to Cats

children.content: Geelong's Tom Atkins convinced umpires a free-kick was his in a decision the AFL has since clarified should have gone Adelaide's way in a tight contest.

image.altText: Tom Atkins and Zac Taylor contest a ball on the ground near their feet

thumbnail.altText: A football on the ground during Hawthorn Hawks' training session at Waverley Park in 2008.

items.title: 'It's about fairness': Australian Iranians hit back at 'racist' visa ban

children.content: Thousands of Iranian travellers have been barred from entering Australia under a government ban targeting people who might overstay their visas, prompting criticism by refugee advocates.

image.altText: A family outdoors smiling

items.title: Offshore wind power to save billions of dollars and kilometres of powerlines

children.content: Developing Victoria's wind power sector could save the state money and powerline infrastructure, while providing stability for Eastern Australia's power grid, a new report shows.

image.altText: Turbines in an offshore wind farm in the ocean.

items.title: Porepunkah's darkest chapter explored in comedy festival show

children.content: David Quirk is adamant his new stand-up show, Porepunkah, won't make fun of last year's police shooting, but it wouldn't exist without it.

image.altText: A man on a stage

items.title: Complaints about foul smells skyrocket in Melbourne's west

children.content: Adam Pattison spent two years doing up his backyard to enjoy outdoor barbecues, but says "sickening" smells kill the vibe five times a week.

image.altText: Adam Pattison looking annoyed in his backyard

items.title: Fire-ravaged shire demands fossil fuel companies foot climate disaster bill

children.content: A Victorian council still cleaning up after a bushfire that wiped out more than 50 homes and businesses wants fossil fuel companies to pay for climate disaster recovery.

items.title: Adrian Portelli's company found guilty of unlawful lotteries by SA court

children.content: Melbourne investor Adrian Portelli has been found not guilty of illegal lottery charges but his company has been ordered to pay tens of thousands of dollars in fines.
